title: Day 40: Succession Vacuum Enters Seventh Week; O3 Collective Council Holds; El Jardinero Still at Large
detail: Day 40 since El Mencho's killing at Tapalpa, Jalisco (February 22, 2026). The CJNG succession vacuum enters its seventh week with organizational cohesion maintained under O3 (Juan Carlos Valencia González) and a collective council structure backed by Grupo Élite's paramilitary capacity. Two senior CJNG commanders reportedly stood aside rather than contest O3's takeover, preserving the cartel's integrity through the critical early succession period — consistent with U.S. NCTC, Wall Street Journal, and InSight Crime reporting. El Jardinero (Audias Flores Silva) remains the primary unresolved high-value targeting objective: his $5 million U.S. State Department bounty is active, and his last confirmed escape from authorities was November 22, 2023 in Zapopan. Narcocorridos (cartel ballads) continue naming El Jardinero as a successor figure — a cultural intelligence signal analysts interpret as reflecting residual internal power tension. Heraclio Guerrero Martínez ('Tío Lako') continues logistics operations in northern and central Mexico. No new senior-command-level captures publicly confirmed for Day 40. Operativo Enjambre cumulative toll holds at 64+ officials detained for cartel ties across multiple states. U.S.-Mexico JITC-CC intelligence-sharing framework remains high-tempo.

title: Operativo Semana Santa Segura 2026: 10,000+ Security Forces Active Across Mexico Including CJNG Zones
detail: Mexico's federal security apparatus activated 'Operativo Semana Santa Segura 2026' (March 27–April 12), deploying 10,000+ security personnel in Mexico City alone and comparable deployments across CJNG-affected states including Jalisco, Michoacán, Colima, and Nayarit. The holiday security operation runs concurrent with ongoing post-Mencho pressure operations and World Cup security preparations — creating overlapping security deployments in Guadalajara, where 2,500+ troops are already permanently stationed. The Semana Santa operation covers tourist zones, coastal areas (Mazatlán, Puerto Vallarta, Manzanillo, Acapulco), Magic Towns, archaeological sites, and federal highway corridors. 742 security vehicles and 23 million projected highway crossings are included in federal planning. CJNG's traditional Semana Santa behavior has historically included tactical restraint in tourist zones to protect cartel financial interests in the tourism and timeshare sectors — though local plaza bosses may independently conduct low-level extortion and trafficking operations. Security analysts note the overlap of Semana Santa, World Cup buildup, and CJNG succession instability as a unique stress test for Mexico's security architecture in Q2 2026.

media.caption: Operativo Semana Santa Segura 2026 deploys thousands of security forces across Mexico including CJNG-affected states
